assertions.title: Browser-only preset yields browser-attributed outcomes only

steps.observed: Terminal reached after ~40 seconds with status: partial

steps.action: Verify ATS sources have zero counters and explicit exclusion warnings
steps.expected: ATS leadsSeen=0, leadsAccepted=0 with 'excluded by preset' warnings
steps.observed: greenhouse: leadsSeen=0, leadsAccepted=0, warning='Source greenhouse was excluded by preset browser_only and did not execute.'; ashby: leadsSeen=0, leadsAccepted=0, warning='Source ashby was excluded by preset browser_only and did not execute.'

steps.action: Verify writeResult shows no ATS-attributed outputs
steps.expected: appended=0, updated=0 (no mutations from any source)

analysis.executedAttributionSemantics: Using orchestrator-specified executed-attribution semantics (not raw source entry presence)
analysis.ATSExclusion: ATS lanes (greenhouse, ashby) correctly excluded via warnings
analysis.ATSCounters: ATS leadsSeen=0, leadsAccepted=0 - no outputs produced
analysis.browserLane: grounded_web attempted but returned no usable candidate links (also 0 leads)
analysis.attributionConclusion: No ATS-attributed outputs produced. Browser lane attempted but found no candidates. Zero sheet mutations confirms no outputs from any lane.

summary: VAL-CROSS-001 PASSES under correct executed-attribution semantics. Fresh browser_only run (run_eb11e5071b984b33b6e27232f7e97a98) shows: (1) ATS lanes correctly excluded with explicit warnings, (2) ATS counters are zero (leadsSeen=0, leadsAccepted=0), (3) no ATS-attributed outputs were produced, (4) writeResult shows zero mutations. This confirms the orchestrator note: excluded ATS entries with zero counters and explicit warnings are valid skip evidence and do NOT violate browser_only outcomes.
